title: The One With The Cuffs

jokes.text: Oh, no. I'm paddling away.
jokes.setup: Someone excitedly announcing free stuff from Treeger
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate rejection using canoe metaphor for escaping junk
jokes.explanation: Nice visual metaphor with good economy, captures Rachel's occasional snobbishness

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Treeger's giving away free stuff!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm not taking it. I don't care if he's giving away a lifetime supply of free stuff, I'm not taking it. That's how it starts, and then before you know it, you're stuck in a canoe with a guy who smells like old cheese.

jokes.text: Really? We got all this rusty crap for free?
jokes.setup: Previous excitement about getting 'cool stuff'
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt assessment of the junk quality
jokes.explanation: Straightforward ironic deflation, functional but not special

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, 'cool stuff' is just a nice way of saying 'junk we don't need'.

jokes.text: This and a bunch of bubble wrap. And some of it is not even popped.
jokes.setup: Listing free items they received
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's genuine excitement about unpopped bubble wrap as a prize
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - finding genuine joy in the mundane, well-integrated character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! I got unpopped bubble wrap!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: This is the best day of my life!

jokes.text: Could we be more white trash?
jokes.setup: The group surrounded by free junk
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware observation about their situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler syntax with good self-awareness, very economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Look at us. We're like a bunch of raccoons going through garbage, except the garbage is free and we're pretending it's treasure.

jokes.text: How desperate am I? Good thing Chandler's not here. He always wins at this game.
jokes.setup: Joey apparently playing some kind of game
jokes.punchline: Joey acknowledging his own desperation while referencing Chandler's superiority
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's self-awareness and competitive dynamic with Chandler

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know I'm desperate, but I'm not Chandler desperate.

jokes.text: I just told my mom I'd cater her party. How come? I need the money. It'd be a great way to get rid of that last smidgen of self-respect.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining why she took a catering job for her mother
jokes.punchline: The dry observation about destroying her remaining self-respect
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific humor about Monica's complex relationship with her mother, well-structured with good word choice ('smidgen')

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I figured if I'm going to destroy my remaining self-respect, I might as well get paid for it.

jokes.text: You don't have to stick up for her. She can't hear you.
jokes.setup: Someone defending Monica's mother's hiring decision
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact dismissal of the need for diplomacy
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery with perfect economy, shows Monica's directness

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, my mother hired who she wanted to hire.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She doesn't need diplomacy, she needs a nap.

jokes.text: Do you have any juice? Just pickle.
jokes.setup: Normal request for juice
jokes.punchline: Chandler only having pickle juice available
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical two-word punchline, absurd but believable for bachelor lifestyle

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey Chandler, you got any juice?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure. I've got pickle juice.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Pickle juice? Why do you have pickle juice?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I like to drink it straight from the jar. It's refreshing.

jokes.text: Last time you went out with her you said she was a dud! I judged her too quickly. This time, we took it to the next level.
jokes.setup: Rachel pointing out Chandler's previous negative assessment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's euphemistic explanation for changing his mind
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo, competent but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You said you'd never get married.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know what I said. But that was before I realized that Monica was willing to overlook all my glaring personality flaws.

jokes.text: It's not an everyday occurrence! Usually I'm in there by myself.
jokes.setup: Context about Chandler's workplace situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler oversharing about his usual solitary office activities
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with sexual implication, fits Chandler's oversharing tendencies

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know, I spend most of my day at work doing... well, let's just say I'm very familiar with the inside of my office door. I've memorized every wood grain. I could describe the lock mechanism to a locksmith. I once spent twenty minutes watching dust particles float through the sunlight. That's not sad at all, right? No, completely normal. Most people probably do the same thing. Stare at walls. Count ceiling tiles. I'm at 247, by the way. I'll let you know when I hit 250. We'll celebrate. Just me and my office supplies.

jokes.text: Sorry. I knew what he meant.
jokes.setup: Chandler's innuendo about his boss
jokes.punchline: Rachel's uncomfortable acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat, functional but not particularly memorable

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My boss kept saying how much he liked my... presentation skills.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Chandler, that's... I mean, that's not...

jokes.text: I thought it was cute. It's what your grandmother's hands looked like when we found her.
jokes.setup: Monica defending her blue nail polish choice
jokes.punchline: Mother's brutally dark comparison to dead grandmother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that's both shocking and perfectly in character for Monica's mother. The casual delivery makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mom, it's just nail polish. I like the color blue.

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: Blue? Monica, you look like your grandmother after the funeral home couldn't get the formaldehyde stains out.

jokes.text: I assume you didn't sleep with anyone there. At least that would be something.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Monica catering a lesbian wedding
jokes.punchline: Mother's cutting remark about Monica's lack of romantic success
jokes.explanation: Devastating maternal put-down that's perfectly in character, great economy and timing

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: A lesbian wedding? Well, at least you'll finally get to cater an event where the bride actually wants to be there.

jokes.text: And hey, it's cool if you're a lesbian.
jokes.setup: Previous conversation about lesbian wedding catering
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's well-meaning but misplaced reassurance to Monica
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe moment - trying to be supportive but missing the point entirely

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Monica. I'm sure your catering will be great. I mean, just because it's for a lesbian wedding doesn't mean anything has to be different. Women like food just as much as men do!

jokes.text: No, but try the classifieds. People sell everything in there.
jokes.setup: Encyclopedia salesman asking if Joey owns encyclopedias
jokes.punchline: Joey helpfully directing the salesman to buy encyclopedias elsewhere
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment showing his helpful nature and complete misunderstanding of sales situation

dialogue.speaker: Encyclopedia Salesman
dialogue.line: Do you own a set of encyclopedias?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, I don't.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But you should try the building next door.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, it's totally unconstitutional. I think he deserves a Nobel Prize. It was like the Algonquin kids' table.
jokes.setup: Salesman asking about feeling left out of conversations
jokes.punchline: Joey's attempt at sophisticated discussion with mixed-up references
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Joey trying to sound smart but getting everything wrong, especially 'Algonquin kids' table'

dialogue.speaker: Salesman
dialogue.line: Do you ever feel left out of conversations?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, all the time! So I've been working on my vocabulary. Like, I'll say things like 'that's a flagrant disregard for the Geneva Convention' or 'that's a Shakespearean tragedy.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The other day, Chandler was talking about some economic thing, and I said, 'Yeah, well, that's just like the stock market crash of 1929 — very Dickensian.'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, the stock market crash happened in 1929, but Dickens died in 1870.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he's dead now!

jokes.text: I meant, it's weird that you only have nine now.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining fake nails, Phoebe calling them weird
jokes.punchline: Phoebe pointing out Monica is missing a nail
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the nail-in-quiche plot, economical and observational

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, where's your nail?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? It broke.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're a freak!

jokes.text: It's because they're going to eat... That's the problem.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting buying new nails so no one will know
jokes.punchline: Monica's horrified realization about the nail in the food
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Monica's dawning horror at the food safety issue

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We could buy new nails so no one will know.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, there's a nail in the food!

jokes.text: And whoever finds it wins the prize!
jokes.setup: Monica confessing about the nail in the quiche
jokes.punchline: Mother treating food contamination as a fun game
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor turning a crisis into a party game, perfectly matches the mother's character

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mom, there's a nail in the quiche.

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: A nail? Oh, how fun! It's like a surprise present in every bite!

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: I once served a casserole with a thumbtack in it. No one even noticed!

jokes.text: You bet I'd lose a nail? Don't be silly. I just bet that I'd need these. You bet that I'd screw up?
jokes.setup: Mother revealing she had backup lasagnas ready
jokes.punchline: Monica's growing realization of her mother's lack of faith
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's hurt feelings and mother-daughter dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, you had backup lasagnas? How many lasagnas did you make?

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Well, I made three just in case.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: In case of what? In case I ruined the first one? In case I ruined the second one?

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: I just like to be prepared.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You made three lasagnas because you didn't think I could make one lasagna.

jokes.text: This was just in case you 'pulled a Monica'. You promised Dr. Weinberg you'd never use that phrase.
jokes.setup: Mother explaining the backup plan
jokes.punchline: Revelation that 'pulling a Monica' is an established family phrase requiring therapy
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke that creates instant backstory - the family has a phrase for Monica's failures that's so hurtful it required therapy intervention

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: So if things don't work out with Chandler, you'll have a backup plan, right?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mom, I'm not going to have a backup plan.

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: Well, you should. You know, in case you pull a Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Pull a Monica? What does that even mean?

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: Oh honey, it's what we call it in the family when you mess everything up and need therapy to deal with it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's... that's a thing?

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: Of course it's a thing. We've been saying it for years.

jokes.text: That's right. My mom has no faith in me! That's hilarious!
jokes.setup: Mother telling Monica to have a sense of humor about herself
jokes.punchline: Monica's bitter sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Good bitter callback showing Monica's justified anger at the family dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, I have a sense of humor about myself. I just don't think it's funny that I'm not funny.

jokes.text: No. You have lasagnas!
jokes.setup: Mother claiming to have faith in Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica's angry correction pointing to the physical evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and structure - the lasagnas become evidence against the mother's claimed faith

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mother
dialogue.line: I have faith in you, Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mom, look around! There are dirty dishes in the sink, there's dust on the shelves, and I haven't vacuumed in weeks!

jokes.text: It just doesn't feel like we're breaking up. No, we are. I'm sad.
jokes.setup: Joanna questioning if they're really breaking up
jokes.punchline: Chandler's unconvincing monotone declaration of sadness
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery showing Chandler's inability to fake emotion convincingly

dialogue.speaker: Joanna
dialogue.line: So we're really breaking up?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes. I'm very sad about this.

jokes.text: When I walk outside naked, people throw garbage.
jokes.setup: Joanna asking why he's getting dressed
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-deprecating explanation for needing clothes
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor with vivid absurd imagery, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Joanna
dialogue.line: Why are you getting dressed?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, they won't let me back in the office without clothes. Apparently, I can't just come to work in my underwear anymore. Something about 'professional standards' and 'making people uncomfortable.' I tried to explain that I'm doing them a favor—I'm a lot less attractive with clothes on.

jokes.text: You're not the boss of me. Yeah, you are.
jokes.setup: Joanna asking Chandler to put his hands together
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate realization and correction
jokes.explanation: Great economy and structure - the quick reversal works perfectly for the power dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Joanna
dialogue.line: Put your hands together.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh! I thought you said put your pants together.

jokes.text: I'll keep it in my butt with your nose.
jokes.setup: Joanna bringing back a macaroon for handcuffed Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's frustrated vulgar retort about storage options
jokes.explanation: Decent frustrated outburst humor, slightly crude but fits the situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Where am I supposed to put it?

jokes.text: I'm in my boss' car. What? Tunnel!
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to explain why he can't come back
jokes.punchline: The obvious lie about being in a tunnel when caught
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom lie trope, functional but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't come back right now.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm in a tunnel.

jokes.text: And on a totally separate subject, that's a lovely pantsuit.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking Rachel to unlock his handcuffs
jokes.punchline: His transparent attempt to change subjects with obvious flattery
jokes.explanation: Good awkward humor with the transparent subject change, fits Chandler's desperate situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Rachel, could you unlock these handcuffs?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: How did you get handcuffed?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's not important. But while you're here, has anyone ever told you that you have the most beautiful eyes in the entire world?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Chandler...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And your hair! My God, your hair is like spun gold!

jokes.text: I did break up with her! She just took it really, really well!
jokes.setup: Rachel angry that Chandler didn't break up with Joanna
jokes.punchline: Chandler's technical defense of his failed breakup
jokes.explanation: Good defensive humor showing how Chandler's breakup attempt backfired

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You didn't break up with her?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, technically I did. I said 'I think we should see other people.' That's breaking up.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Chandler, she's still your girlfriend!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, she didn't say she agreed with me!

jokes.text: It did enter my mind. But then something happened that made it shoot right out.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if jeopardizing her career entered his mind
jokes.punchline: Chandler's euphemistic explanation for losing focus
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo, competent but not particularly creative

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Did it ever enter your mind that you were jeopardizing my career?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, there are a lot of things that enter my mind, but I try not to act on all of them.

jokes.text: You must have me confused with the 'Amazing Chandler'!
jokes.setup: Rachel telling him to get himself out of handcuffs
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic reference to himself as a magician
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor with perfect character voice and economy

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Get yourself out of the handcuffs.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh sure, I'll just use my amazing powers of magic.

jokes.text: I never borrowed it. Then I lost it. Buy me one!
jokes.setup: Rachel demanding her Walkman back
jokes.punchline: Chandler's increasingly desperate bargaining logic
jokes.explanation: Great escalating desperation with absurd logic progression, very economical

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Give me back my Walkman!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, but hear me out. What if I just... borrow it for one more day?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No! Give it back now!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Fine, fine. What if I keep it for two more days, and in return, I'll... do your laundry?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. Three days, I'll do your laundry AND make you lunch for a week.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's it, I'm calling Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait! How about forever, and I buy you a new, better Walkman? A Discman even!

jokes.text: No, I always see guys do this when handcuffs are taken off.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if his wrists hurt
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact reference to his extensive handcuff experience
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor with slight innuendo about his arrest history

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Do your wrists hurt?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, they would if I hadn't gotten so good at getting out of handcuffs.

jokes.text: I'll tell a lie! I'm good at lying! I actually did borrow your Walkman!
jokes.setup: Need to explain his absence to Joanna
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately proving he's bad at lying by confessing
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-defeating humor where Chandler immediately undermines his claim about being good at lying

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I couldn't come in today because... my grandmother died.

dialogue.speaker: Joanna
dialogue.line: Oh Chandler, I'm so sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, she's going to be really upset she missed work.

jokes.text: He cut off his ear. And? I'm out.
jokes.setup: Encyclopedia salesman asking about Van Gogh
jokes.punchline: Joey's single fact exhausting his knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - confidently sharing his one fact then immediately admitting defeat

dialogue.speaker: Salesman
dialogue.line: Did you know Van Gogh only sold one painting in his lifetime?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wow. So... he really struggled, huh?

dialogue.speaker: Salesman
dialogue.line: Yes, he was very poor and—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I got it. Van Gogh: poor guy, didn't sell paintings. Anything else?

dialogue.speaker: Salesman
dialogue.line: Well, he also cut off his own ear—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. That's a whole different thing.

jokes.text: I thought he cut off his ear because he sucked.
jokes.setup: Looking at Van Gogh painting
jokes.punchline: Joey's backwards logic about artistic motivation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic - completely backwards understanding of artistic struggle, very quotable and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't get it. Why would he cut off his ear? If I really loved my girlfriend, I'd buy her a nice present, not mutilate myself.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Besides, what's she gonna do with an ear? It's not like she can wear it.

jokes.text: Where does the Pope live? In the woods.
jokes.setup: Salesman testing Joey's knowledge
jokes.punchline: Joey confusing the Pope with bears or other woodland creatures
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Joey's confused knowledge base

dialogue.speaker: Salesman
dialogue.line: So Joey, who's the head of the Catholic Church?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The Pope.

dialogue.speaker: Salesman
dialogue.line: Right. And what do you call a large furry animal that lives in the woods?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A bear?

dialogue.speaker: Salesman
dialogue.line: Exactly. So what's the difference?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: One wears a funny hat?

jokes.text: Spock's birth control.
jokes.setup: Question about vulcanized rubber
jokes.punchline: Joey connecting 'vulcanized' to Vulcans in Star Trek
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining Joey's pop culture knowledge with sexual humor, incredibly economical and quotable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Vulcanized? Like the Vulcans from Star Trek?

jokes.text: Why does that have to be a bad thing? Just change what it means.
jokes.setup: Monica upset about 'pulling a Monica' phrase
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's optimistic solution to redefine the term
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment showing her positive outlook and simple solutions to complex problems

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! You know what? We could start a new thing where 'pulling a Monica' means doing something really smart and organized!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah! Like, 'Oh, I totally pulled a Monica and color-coded my sock drawer by thread count!'

jokes.text: A fireman saves a baby, and they go, 'He pulled a Monica'.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's plan to rebrand 'pulling a Monica'
jokes.punchline: Specific example of heroic Monica reference
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the rebranding theme with specific heroic example

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know what? We should start using 'pulling a Monica' as a compliment. Like when someone cleans up after themselves or organizes something really well.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really? I like that.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah! Like, 'Oh my God, I totally pulled a Monica and color-coded my spice rack by cuisine and alphabetical order.'

jokes.text: Someone hits a homer, and they say, 'That one's out of here'. Because some things don't change.
jokes.setup: Pattern of rebranding examples
jokes.punchline: Phoebe breaking the pattern with normal baseball commentary
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection humor breaking the established pattern with realistic observation

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: See, that's what I'm talking about. They rebrand everything. 'New Coke,' 'New Diet Coke,' 'Coke Zero'... it's all just Coke!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And don't even get me started on 'The Learning Channel.' It's not about learning anymore, it's about watching people hoard stuff and fight their families.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: But baseball... baseball stays the same. Batter up! There's a fastball on the inside corner. He swings and... it's a fly ball to left field. The outfielder is moving back... back... he's got it!

jokes.text: My breasts are really strong.
jokes.setup: Monica apparently struggling with something physical
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific boast about breast strength
jokes.explanation: Bizarre and memorable line, very quotable despite being somewhat random

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can crack walnuts with my breasts.

jokes.text: I'll clean your bathroom for a month. Still won't be clean.
jokes.setup: Chandler offering to clean Rachel's bathroom as bribe
jokes.punchline: Rachel's cutting assessment of his cleaning abilities
jokes.explanation: Good quick insult with perfect economy and timing

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Chandler, the last time you cleaned a bathroom, I found a Cheeto behind the toilet.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That was... a decorative Cheeto.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It had mold on it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Vintage.

jokes.text: Why won't you hear me! Sophie, help me! Sophie, sit!
jokes.setup: Chandler's desperation reaching a peak
jokes.punchline: His appeal to a dog followed by immediate training command
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chandler's desperation turning to dog commands

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more desperate?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Here boy, here boy! Please, I'm begging you!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Sit!

jokes.text: There's a lot I didn't know about vomit.
jokes.setup: Joey apparently reading encyclopedia entry
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact assessment of learning about vomit
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery with perfect Joey voice - genuine fascination with gross topics

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Did you know that vomit can travel up to 27 feet? I just learned that. And honestly, it's not something you want to know. It's one of those things where ignorance is bliss. But now I know. So that's great.

jokes.text: I'm home during the day...and I got patio furniture in my living room.
jokes.setup: Salesman asking about $1200 for encyclopedias
jokes.punchline: Joey's evidence of his obvious poverty
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware poverty humor with specific visual details that paint the picture

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A thousand two hundred dollars?! For books?! Do I look like a guy who has a thousand two hundred dollars?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't even afford the 'D' volume!

jokes.text: I got a baby Tootsie Roll...a movie stub, keys, a Kleenex...a rock...and an army man.
jokes.setup: Showing what he has in his pockets
jokes.punchline: The pathetic inventory of random pocket items
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list showing Joey's poverty through specific childlike items

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Look what I found in my pockets today. A gum wrapper, three pennies, a button that's not from any of my shirts, and what I think used to be a sandwich.

jokes.text: And a fifty. These must be Chandler's pants.
jokes.setup: Joey showing his pathetic possessions
jokes.punchline: Finding money and immediately realizing it's not his clothes
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Joey logic - money means it can't be his

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh wow, look at all this money I found!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... these aren't my clothes.

jokes.text: I want to see how this bad boy turns out.
jokes.setup: Choosing to buy volume V of encyclopedia
jokes.punchline: Joey treating an encyclopedia like a suspenseful story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - treating factual reference material like entertainment, shows his unique worldview

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I can't wait to see what happens next! Do you think Ross will finally tell Rachel how he feels?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait... it says here that the Earth revolves around the sun. I did NOT see that coming!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: This is better than the movies! Volume V, baby! The plot thickens!

jokes.text: I can be very generous...or very stingy.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining how she'll describe what she saw
jokes.punchline: Her euphemistic threat about describing Chandler's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good power dynamic humor with clear sexual innuendo, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm going to tell everyone about your... situation.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What situation?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Let's just say when you go to the bathroom, even the tiles feel inadequate.

jokes.text: I can make you this generation's Milton Berle. And Milton Berle has... Not compared to you.
jokes.setup: Rachel offering to make Chandler a legend
jokes.punchline: The implication about Milton Berle's legendary endowment being small next to Chandler
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo with historical reference, though requires cultural knowledge to fully land

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm going to make you a legend.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How? By telling people that Milton Berle's is small next to mine?

jokes.text: A bitch? I meant 'wrong', but we can use your word.
jokes.setup: Monica asking what successful catering would make her mother
jokes.punchline: Monica's hostile interpretation vs. mother's diplomatic correction
jokes.explanation: Good tension between Monica's hostility and mother's diplomatic response, nice wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What would make you proud? If I had a successful catering business?

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: Well, that would be wonderful, but I was thinking more along the lines of you getting married and having children.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So what you're saying is my catering business will never be successful enough.

dialogue.speaker: Monica's Mom
dialogue.line: No, that's not what I'm saying at all.

jokes.text: You might even say that she 'pulled a Monica'.
jokes.setup: Monica's successful catering job
jokes.punchline: Phoebe using the phrase positively as planned
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the rebranding plan, shows Phoebe following through on her idea

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Monica, your catering business is doing so well! You're like, totally killing it!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Thanks, Phoebe. I've been working really hard.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, seriously, you're absolutely crushing it! You're crushing it so hard right now!

jokes.text: She doesn't know we switched it.
jokes.setup: Mother praising Monica's successful catering
jokes.punchline: Monica revealing they secretly used the backup lasagna
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation showing Monica's practical solution, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Thank you so much for saying that. The lasagna was actually... well, it was the backup lasagna.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: The first one kind of... didn't make it. But nobody could tell the difference!

jokes.text: I love you. What's going on? No, he doesn't!
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to get out of handcuffs
jokes.punchline: His desperate declaration followed by Rachel's immediate denial
jokes.explanation: Standard desperation humor with quick denial, functional but not special

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can get out of these handcuffs!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, you can't.

jokes.text: Speaking of volcanoes, man, are they a violent igneous rock formation!
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Vesuvius
jokes.punchline: Joey showing off his new encyclopedia knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey actually learned from his purchase, character growth humor

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Vesuvius? Oh yeah, that's a volcano. It erupted in 79 A.D. and buried Pompeii. I read about it in this encyclopedia I got.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? I'm not just a pretty face, you know. I'm expanding my mind.

jokes.text: Why are you talking about volcanoes all of a sudden? We don't have to. What do you want to talk about? Vivisection? The vas deferens? The Vietnam War?
jokes.setup: Monica questioning Joey's sudden volcano expertise
jokes.punchline: Joey's rapid-fire list of V-volume topics
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Joey's limited but enthusiastic knowledge base, perfect character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, since when are you an expert on volcanoes?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not just about volcanoes, Mon. I know about Vikings, violins, vitamins, volleyball, ventriloquism, vacuums, and... Vegas!

jokes.text: Could there be more Kims?
jokes.setup: Joey discussing Korea
jokes.punchline: His observation about the prevalence of the name Kim
jokes.explanation: Good Joey observation mixing his learning with his typical focus on simple patterns

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know, in Korea, like, everybody's name is Kim. It's like, how do they even know who they're talking to?

jokes.text: I've had a slight office mishap.
jokes.setup: Joanna found handcuffed to her office chair
jokes.punchline: Her massive understatement of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor showing professional dignity in embarrassing situation

dialogue.speaker: Joanna
dialogue.line: Well, this is a little awkward.

tmdb_overview: Monica caters a party for her mother, who still doesn't quite know how to talk to her daughter. Chandler's encounter with Rachel's boss leads to an embarrassing situation. And an encyclopedia salesman takes advantage of Joey.
insight: 60 jokes in 22 minutes, but weak impact—character work can't sustain mid-tier humor density.
